The first author affiliation is University of Wisconsin in the United States and the field sites are in the United States. The methods provide coordinates for Glacier and Greater Yellowstone and the average of those coordinates is reported. Fires occurred between 1994 and 2003 and seedlings were sampled in 2013 so the observation window spans up to about 20 years which is 7300 days. Fire is treated as discrete events with post-fire years referenced which matches a pulse disturbance framing.

The study states that it used standardized impact and absolute recovery rates as proxies for ecological resilience to drought and it quantified recovery rates as the absolute change in grass greenness per day after each dry season. Grass greenness was measured monthly during dry and wet seasons of 2018 and 2019 in permanent plots under canopies and paired open plots which is direct observational data of system response across seasonal drought and subsequent wet season. The framework is not defined as an equation including resilience because resilience is only mentioned as a concept and as proxies without an explicit component framework. The quantification requested for resilience is recovery rate because resilience was proxied by recovery rates and calculated per unit time after the dry season. The target variable used for resilience quantification is grass greenness which the paper describes as a response variable and a physiological condition indicator for grasslands. The level is ecosystem because the resilience metric is based on plot level grassland condition rather than individual organisms or a single population. The approach is field observation because the work is described as a correlational study under natural conditions in cattle farms with repeated field monitoring. The first author affiliation is Wageningen University and Research in The Netherlands. The field data were collected in the municipality of Tizimín in Yucatán in the southeast of Mexico. Coordinates are not provided in the main text and the only mention is that farm coordinates are in Table S1 which is not included here so location and range are NA. The habitat is agricultural because the study system is actively used cattle pastures established by logging and burning dry forests and planted with exotic grasses. The taxon is Plantae because resilience was quantified from grass greenness. The disturbance is unmanipulated climatic drought because drought severity and seasonal precipitation variation were observed not imposed. The disturbance pattern is press because drought is treated as a prolonged seasonal condition with recovery assessed at the onset of the rainy season rather than a discrete experimental event. Observation duration spans from the first monitoring in March 2018 to the last monitoring in September 2019 which is about 550 days.

The introduction states resilience is evaluated as the rate a variable returns to its reference condition after a perturbation and the study also evaluates resilience sensu Holling as whether altered states form under changing disturbance regimes. The methods describe a 19 year dataset of pre and post fire vegetation change from 55 permanent plots established in 1999 and remeasured after the 2005 fire in 2007 2010 2015 and 2018 to 2019. Results explicitly label resilience as rate of return to pre fire demographic rates and quantify growth and mortality trajectories through time using generalized estimating equations. The study also quantifies persistence of altered states using understorey community composition dissimilarity and discusses tipping into alternate stable states. Target variables used in resilience analyses include annual diameter growth rate and probability of mortality and understorey woody community composition recovery relative to pre fire composition. The disturbance includes the 2005 fire described as an agricultural fire that escaped into the parks and severe droughts defined using PDSI thresholds with post fire drought periods. Fire is treated as a discrete event with post fire recovery while drought is treated as multi month periods during the post fire years. The study site is in the Cordillera Central in two national parks in the Dominican Republic and plot elevations span 1695 to 3050 m a s l. The first author affiliation is University of Denver in the USA. The habitat is a tropical montane pine forest dominated by Pinus occidentalis. The focal organisms are plants including pine trees and shrub taxa. The observation window spans from 1999 to 2018 to 2019 which is about 20 years.

The introduction states that mangrove driven increases in grazing may increase the likelihood that corals will recover from disturbance and enhance their rate of recovery and the study tests whether connectivity can enhance the resilience of corals to climate driven disturbance. The methods state that a simulation model of coral reef dynamics was used and that acute disturbance occurs from hurricanes and scenarios simulated major hurricane events with long term incidence of once per decade and once every 20 years. The methods state that the net outcome of hurricane induced disturbance and recovery on total coral cover was evaluated after a 50 year simulation and results are reported for total coral cover. The discussion states that mangrove based ontogenetic migrations of parrotfish may through a trophic cascade on macroalgae enhance the recovery rate of mid shelf reefs from hurricanes. Therefore resilience is treated as recovery rate and quantified from modelled recovery dynamics in total coral cover after hurricane disturbance. The target variable used for resilience evaluation is total coral cover which is a cover variable and it is a community level state because it aggregates corals. The first author affiliation is University of Exeter UK. Field data used for parameterization and grazing structure are stated as from Glovers Atoll Belize and from Belize Bonaire and the Bahamas and the model was tested against an independent 20 year empirical dataset from Jamaica. The shallow reef macroalgae patch monitoring was at Glovers Atoll Belize and Fig 2 refers to Glovers Reef Belize so the location is represented by Glovers Atoll Belize. Hurricanes are treated as acute events implemented stochastically so the disturbance pattern is pulse and the disturbance type is climatic. The simulation duration is 50 years which is 18250 days.

The study quantified responses to heatwaves by monitoring mussel survival rates under constant heatwave conditions in a laboratory experiment and under dynamic heatwave scenarios simulated in an outdoor mesocosm experiment. The thermal tolerance landscape model predicted population survival trajectories and these predictions were compared directly with observed survival rates using Kaplan–Meier curves and mean absolute deviance. The disturbance was imposed by the researchers by exposing mussels to constant temperatures of 26 to 29C and by simulating dynamic heatwaves by increasing baseline temperatures by 2.5C and later returning to ambient fjord temperatures. Resilience was discussed as the mussel population response to extreme heatwaves and was not defined as part of an explicit equation framework that includes resilience. The quantified response focused on survival probability declines and mortality under heatwave exposure which reflects the ability to deviate less from the original living state under disturbance. The target variable used for the core resilience quantification was survival probability and survival rate which are demographic parameters. The focal organism was the blue mussel Mytilus from the Mytilus edulis complex which is Animalia. The first author affiliation is GEOMAR Helmholtz Centre for Ocean Research Kiel in Germany. Mussels were collected near GEOMAR at 54°19′45.4″N 10°08′56.2″E and the system used non filtered seawater from the fjord indicating a shallow coastal marine setting. The dynamic heatwave experiment duration was described as a 64 day dynamic heatwave experiment and survival outcomes were reported after 64 days. The heatwave manipulation was maintained across the experiment window rather than a single brief event which matches a press disturbance pattern.

The study states that it constructed an indicator system of farmland ecosystem resilience supported by 30 specific indicators and that it measures and analyzes Ethiopia’s FER at an annual time scale from 2003 to 2018. The FER is calculated as FER = HD + ND + GD + RF + RFS + RFM + RS with each level-1 indicator computed from weighted level-2 indicators including population density flood index drought index wind speed temperatures elevation slope EVI NPP and multiple socioeconomic variables. This matches an inferred measurement because resilience is evaluated from an indicator system and composite index rather than directly quantified as recovery dynamics of a single observed state variable after a defined disturbance. The paper explicitly says it considers resilience as part of sustainability and that this gives resilience an ability for assessing sustainability which supports the framework sustainability = resilience. Because measurement is inferred quantification is inferred. The resilience index includes land cover derived disturbance metrics landscape fragmentation splitting index and perimeter area fractal dimension and it uses a farmland distribution base map from MODIS land cover which supports Land use as the target variable type for inferred resilience. The analysis is for Ethiopia and the introduction gives Ethiopia (6–9 N 34–40 E) so field country is ET and an average coordinate of N7.5 E37 is used with a small range so location range is 0. The first author affiliation is in Beijing China so institution country is CN. Data sources are remote sensing and global datasets applied to Ethiopia without an experiment so the approach is field observation. Because level is landscape taxon is NA and because measurement is inferred disturbance type pattern and observation duration are NA.

The study quantifies urban ecological resilience using complex network robustness and connectivity metrics rather than measuring recovery dynamics of an ecosystem state variable through time series. The Methods define robustness as the ability of a complex network to maintain performance and state that robustness is used to investigate ecosystem stability or how resilient it is to disturbances. The robustness analysis is based on simulated random and malicious attacks on nodes and edges and on connectivity robustness and recovery robustness formulas. Habitat connectivity is quantified using IIC and PC and the Results report increases in IIC and PC after URPs. These are regional network parameters derived from an ecological network constructed from ecological sources and corridors. The work is a URPs simulation comparing before and after regeneration and includes predicted NDVI following urban regeneration and scenario based land use modification. The study area is the Nanning City District Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region southern China with coordinates 22.6708 to 22.9028 N and 108.1556 to 108.4322 E and the average coordinate is reported. The land context is an urban area with old urban buildings dominating in the north central region and the analysis concerns urban regeneration programs. Because the resilience quantification is based on network robustness and connectivity proxies rather than observed system recovery time series the measurement is inferred and disturbance fields are not applicable under the instructions.

The study states it examined experimental burning and measured resilience as recovery using system measurements. It defines stability as having two components resistance and resilience and says it examined resistance and resilience. It defines resilience as recovery of the pre-disturbance light levels and also states resilience was the degree of recovery of pre-disturbance light and bare soil using statistical comparison of pre vs post disturbance levels. This corresponds to recovery degree because it is based on the difference between pre disturbance and post disturbance levels rather than a rate or time to return. The variables used to quantify resilience are light and bare soil which are abiotic conditions. The work is a factorial field experiment with plots burned in July and October 2000 and measurements taken before and two years after burning. The first author affiliation is University of British Columbia in Canada. The field site is Cowichan Garry Oak reserve on southeastern Vancouver Island British Columbia Canada. The habitat is described as an oak savanna. The focal organisms are plants and the disturbance is burning applied by the researchers. The burns are discrete events in 2000 and recovery is assessed two years later so the disturbance pattern is pulse. The duration between pre burn and two years after burning is two years which is 730 days.

The introduction defines resilience as high resilience following drought and gives the example rapid recovery and contrasts it with resistance during drought. The methods state that aboveground net primary productivity was measured in 2006 to 2014 prior to the drought and again the year after the extreme drought ended in 2017 and that EVI from 2000 to 2017 was used as a proxy for live vegetation biomass before during and after the drought. The results state that total ANPP recovered to predrought levels during the 2017 growing season and that EVI trends also show full recovery in 2017. The response ratio compares ANPP postdrought in 2017 to ANPP predrought averaged across 2006 to 2014 and confidence intervals overlapping zero indicate no difference from predrought which operationalizes full recovery. This is recovery degree because resilience is quantified as returning to predrought levels rather than a rate or time to recovery. The target variable is ecosystem function because ANPP and EVI are used as productivity proxies and the paper frames resilience as resilience of ecosystem function. The level is ecosystem because total ANPP is an ecosystem productivity measure irrespective of species identity. The approach is field observation because the study measured ANPP in permanent plots and used MODIS EVI time series without imposing drought experimentally. The disturbance is unmanipulated climatic because the study analyzes an exceptional drought during the 2015 and 2016 growing seasons. The disturbance pattern is pulse because the drought is treated as an event that ended and recovery was assessed the year after it ended. The first author affiliation is University of Wyoming in the United States. The field site is Kruger National Park South Africa and the Satara weather station coordinates are given as 24 23 49.33 S and 31 46 39.04 E. The observation duration is from the first ANPP measurements in 2006 to the postdrought ANPP measurement in 2017 which spans 11 years or 4015 days using 365 days per year.

The study states it develops a three-dimensional ecological resilience evaluation index system comprising resistance adaptability and recovery and measures ecological resilience of 76 cities using the entropy method. The indicators used are population density particulate matter concentration industrial wastewater emissions industrial sulfur dioxide emissions social electricity consumption water supply solid waste utilization domestic waste disposal sewage treatment domestic waste removed public transport vehicles green space per capita and greening coverage of built-up areas. These are proxy indicators and not direct time series of ecosystem state recovery after a defined disturbance so resilience is inferred. The framework is explicitly given as ecological resilience comprising resistance adaptability and recovery. Because measurement is inferred quantification is inferred. The target variables used to quantify resilience are socio economic and infrastructure and environmental management indicators so the target variable type is socio-eco parameter. The unit of analysis is 76 cities across the Yellow River Basin and the work analyzes spatial differentiation so the level is landscape. Data are drawn from statistical yearbooks and national data centers and analyzed without experimental manipulation so the approach is field observation. The first author affiliation is Xinyang Normal University in China so institution country is CN. The research region is the Yellow River Basin in China so field country is CN. The paper gives the basin extent as 32•10′N to 41•50′N and 95•53′E to 119•05′E so the average coordinate is N37.0 E107.4833 and the location range is computed from the farthest corner relative to that average. The introduction describes the basin as including plateau desert wetland so habitat type is multi. No organism group is the subject because resilience is computed for cities using socio ecological indicators so taxon is NA. Disturbance is discussed generally as perturbations and human disturbances but not used as a defined disturbance in resilience quantification so disturbance type pattern and observation duration are NA.

The introduction defines resistance as capacity to survive disturbance through persistence and resilience as capacity to recover to pre-disturbance abundance levels through recruitment and it frames these as relative resistance and resilience. The methods state that experimental fires were applied and the identity and density of all woody species were recorded before and after fire in 33 sites. The response variable was relative changes in abundance from pre to post fire represented as response ratios based on post fire density and pre fire density. The results and figures interpret resilience as whether pre fire abundance levels are reached and as self replacement thresholds which is recovery to pre disturbance abundance. The study uses field based experiments with experimental fires and post fire remeasurement 2 years after fire for 2009 burns and 3 years after fire for 2006 to 2008 burns. The study includes rainfall variation as covariates and discusses reduced winter rain after fire and uses fitted lines for 100% and 80% mean winter rainfall which is unmanipulated climatic variation. The study setting is kwongan shrublands near Eneabba in southwestern Australia and the first author affiliation is Murdoch University in Australia. No explicit coordinates are provided in the text for Eneabba or the sites.

The introduction defines stability as predictability persistence in time resistance and resilience and defines resilience as the tendency to return to a prior condition following disturbance. The methods state that to measure resilience the average canonical score of each control at each scale was subtracted from the average value of the corresponding treatment average and the differences were centered by the premanipulation average and plotted across time. The results describe resilience as temporal deviation of a disturbed community from the control assemblage and report that compositional effects were short lived and effectively removed and that by the end of the sample period macroalgal removal had no effect on species composition. These statements indicate resilience is quantified as the degree of return of assemblage composition and density differences toward control and baseline values rather than an explicit rate or time to recovery. The target variables used in the resilience plots are Canonical Discriminant Axis 1 representing assemblage composition and density and abundance. The study uses manipulated disturbances fish removal and macroalgal removal in permanent quadrats on contiguous reef with repeated visual censuses. The study area is the Cape Rodney to Okakari Point Marine Reserve on the northeastern coast of New Zealand with coordinates given as 36 8169S and 1468469 to 49 9E. The fish removal experiment ran from October 1990 to November 1991 which is about 13 months. The macroalgal removal experiment ran from December 1990 to October 1991. The first author affiliation is University of Auckland in New Zealand. The habitat is subtidal rocky reef and kelp forest which is a shallow nearshore marine environment. The focal organisms are blennioid fishes.

The introduction defines resilience as rate of recovery after disturbance and stability as ability of an ecosystem to maintain a steady state. The study applied experimental disturbances of different intensities to Spartina alterniflora stands along a sediment deposition stress gradient. The methods state that plots were sampled three times 9/28/2000 5/9/2001 and 10/30/2001 and that the last day of disturbance 6/30/2000 was time zero to quantify rate of recovery. Resilience in each stable plot was determined as recovery rate using quadratic regression and was summarized as percent recovery month to 50% of controls. The vegetation principal component was defined by above ground biomass number of stems and mean canopy height and Table 2 reports above ground biomass recovery outcomes. The lethal disturbance was Roundup herbicide application and the non lethal disturbance was clipping to the soil surface which mimicked herbivory. The discussion states that after lethal disturbance the no deposition zone did not recover during the study period and remained denuded while high and moderate deposition zones recovered rapidly. The study site coordinates are given as 29 811.850N 89 826.300W near Venice Louisiana and the habitat is a salt marsh. The first author affiliation is Louisiana State University in Baton Rouge USA and the field work occurred in Louisiana USA.

The study experimentally imposed extreme drought and a two-week heat wave over two years in a central U.S. grassland and monitored subsequent recovery one year post-drought. Resilience is defined as capacity for recovery of function and the recovery assessment is based on observed aboveground net primary productivity. The paper states that ANPP recovered completely the year after drought and that they observed complete recovery in ecosystem function total ANPP just one year post-drought. This is a recovery degree quantification because it evaluates whether total ANPP returned to the pre disturbance level rather than estimating a rate parameter. The framework is presented as resistance and resilience in parallel with resistance described as capacity to withstand change and resilience as capacity for recovery of function. The target variable used to quantify resilience is ecosystem function via total ANPP. The work is a field experiment at Konza Prairie Biological Station in northeastern Kansas USA with coordinates given as 39 8050 N 968350 W. The disturbance is manipulated climatic because drought and heat wave treatments were imposed. The disturbance is treated as pulse because heat was added for two weeks and drought was imposed during defined growing seasons and then removed in 2012 to observe recovery. Observation duration spans from establishment in 2010 through the recovery year 2012 with measurements at the end of each growing season giving 2010 to 2012 which is 885 days.

The study experimentally imposed food deprivation by rearing larvae in fed treatments with algae and unfed treatments with filtered seawater only and by initiating delayed feeding at day 12 and day 16. The Methods state they tested resilience by measuring the ability of unfed larvae to sustain respiration conserve lipid and protein reserves maintain protein synthesis and turnover and recover once algal food becomes available. The Results report recovery after prolonged food deprivation measured by increases in respiration protein accretion midline body length and post oral arm length. The Results explicitly quantify recovery as rates stating these recovery rates were within 0.9 fold of the respiration rate and 0.8 fold of protein accretion rate of control larvae. Because resilience is quantified from the system response after food is made available it is observed rather than inferred. The target variables directly used to show recovery are respiration rate oxygen consumption protein accretion protein content and morphometric growth measures midline body length and post oral arm length. Respiration is a functional metabolic rate and protein content is biomass content and body lengths are growth morphology. The work was conducted at the University of Southern California Wrigley Marine Science Center Santa Catalina Island United States and bacteria were collected from Pacific Ocean waters off Santa Catalina Island California. The first author affiliation is the University of Southern California Los Angeles California United States. The disturbance is a manipulated change in food availability described as algal food deprivation and delayed feeding which fits a resource disturbance. The disturbance is treated as continuous nutritional stress for unfed larvae and the text describes continuous nutritional stress and extended periods without algal food rather than a discrete event. The observation window for the main recovery comparison spans from 4 day old start to 22 day old termination giving 18 days.

Framework: resilience = ability to return to a predisturbance state
Justification: The study states that it tested resilience of microbial communities challenged by an antibiotic and that resilience was defined as the ability of a community to return to a predisturbance state. The experiment used chemostats with continuous addition of tetracycline described as a press disturbance and a one time addition of allochthonous Escherichia coli described as a pulse disturbance. Resilience was analysed by comparing community descriptors between day 20 and day 27 including total cell numbers aggregates flagellates and alpha and beta diversity. The text says to evaluate the resilience of the community cell numbers at day 20 and day 27 were compared and it also tested Bray Curtis distance from day 20 to day 27 where a less stable or less resilient community would have resulted in a larger distance. These are direct observations of abundance and community composition and diversity over time so the measurement is observed. The first author affiliation is National Research Council Water Research Institute in Verbania Italy. The lake water used to prepare the medium is from Lake Maggiore with coordinates 45.92247 N and 8.54829 E. The chemostat ran in a climate chamber for 28 days and the first stable state was reached after 4 days so the observation window for stability and disturbance response spans from day 4 to day 27 which is 23 days.

The study states that it evaluated resilience using a long term experiment from 2006 to 2020 and measured community structure before during and after the marine heatwave onset and sea star wasting disease outbreak. Resilience was quantified from observed community structure using Bray Curtis based community similarity described as resilience before to after comparisons of community structure within the same plots. The Methods explicitly define a framework where resilience can be partitioned into resistance and recovery and then state they use resilience as synonymous with recovery. The resilience metric compares after 2017 to 2020 to before 2006 to 2014 and the Discussion notes community structure differed from pre disturbance conditions suggesting low resilience which matches recovery degree rather than recovery rate or recovery time. The target variable for resilience is community structure based on functional group relative abundances and Bray Curtis dissimilarity which is composition. The work is a field experiment with permanent plots and treatments and annual surveys. The first author affiliation is Oregon State University in the United States. The field sites are on the coasts of Oregon and northern California in the United States. The habitat is rocky intertidal benches which fit coastal habitat. The resilience relevant community structure includes macrophytes and sessile invertebrates so both Plantae and Animalia are involved. The disturbances used for the resilience comparison are the unmanipulated marine heatwave and the unmanipulated disease outbreak. The marine heatwave is treated as a multi year event from autumn 2014 to autumn 2016 and disease persisted from 2015 to 2020 so the disturbance pattern is press. The observation window for resilience spans from the first measurements in 2006 to the last in 2020 which is 16 years or 5840 days using 365 days per year.

The introduction states that they investigated resilience of clones following above-ground disturbance in terms of root carbohydrates leaf area renewal and root retention. The methods describe a 2 × 2 factorial experiment where saplings were cut at the soil surface in late October or late May and leaf area renewal was manipulated by allowing either only one or all root suckers to re-grow. The results state that leaf area and leaf area ratio recovered within a year to near pre-treatment levels in the fall cut all suckers treatment and that root starch concentrations had returned to pre-treatment values in that treatment. The discussion states that starch concentrations in the roots of the fall cut saplings with all suckers growing had reached pre-treatment levels by the end of the first season following suckering. These comparisons to pre-treatment values indicate recovery degree after a cutting disturbance. Resilience is discussed directly but no explicit equation framework is provided. The variables used to demonstrate resilience are leaf area renewal and rebuilding of root carbohydrate reserves and these are measured from the plants during the year after cutting. The study is a field experiment because seedlings were transplanted to an open field at the University of Alberta experimental station near Ellerslie and then cut and manipulated. The first author affiliation is University of Alberta in Canada. The field site is near Ellerslie in Alberta Canada and the institution is in Edmonton so the location is approximated to Edmonton area coordinates. The disturbance is manipulated because cutting and sucker removal were imposed by the experiment and it is structural because it removes above-ground structures and alters sprouting structure. The disturbance is a pulse because cutting is a discrete event and recovery was assessed over the following year. The observation duration is one year because post-treatment growth characteristics are reported after 1 year of suckering.

The study conducted ecological vulnerability assessment using a sensitivity-resilience-pressure model and states that the SRP model assesses vulnerability from three dimensions ecological sensitivity ecological resilience and ecological pressure. Resilience is defined as the ability of reverting to original or improved condition after external disturbances and it is represented by indicators including FVC NPP and biological abundance index within the SRP indicator system. The ecological vulnerability index is calculated from 11 indicators using SPCA weights and principal components rather than measuring recovery dynamics of a system state after a disturbance. The data sources are multi source remote sensing and gridded datasets including MODIS products DEM land use and land cover nighttime lights and population density which are used to construct indices rather than observe post disturbance recovery trajectories. The framework figure is described as an EVA framework and Table 1 lists the SRP criteria layers sensitivity resilience pressure which together form the EVA index. The study area is the Loess Plateau with coordinates 33•45′–41•16′N and 100•54′–114•33′E and the average coordinate is reported. The affiliated institution is Chang an University in China. The analysis covers multiple ecological zones and land use types including forestland grassland cropland urban land and sandy desert region so the habitat type is multi. Because measurement is inferred disturbance type disturbance pattern and observation duration are not applicable.

The study directly measures system response by tracking the relative frequencies of Escherichia coli and Saccharomyces cerevisiae through time using flow cytometry during stability assays. It quantifies recovery dynamics because it defines recovery rate as the average days required for replicates displaced at least 20% from equilibrium to reach the equilibrium state and reports that the 4,000 gen community reached equilibrium more quickly. It quantifies invariability because it measures variation in E. coli frequency over time and reports lower variability across replicate trials for the 4,000 gen community. It quantifies latitude because it maps tipping points and the range of coexistence across tetracycline concentrations and defines tipping points as boundaries of bistability and extinction outcomes. Resilience is discussed via basin of attraction metaphors and tipping points but no explicit equation framework including resilience is defined. The target variable used for resilience quantification is species relative frequency which is community composition and its variability and recovery. The work is a laboratory co-culture manipulation with daily dilution and imposed tetracycline gradients and imposed initial frequency perturbations and it also includes simulations using a modified Lotka Volterra model parameterized with experimental data. The first author affiliation is School of Biological Sciences Monash University Clayton Victoria Australia. The experiments were conducted at Monash University including flow cytometry at the FlowCore facility at Monash University. No explicit coordinates are provided so the location is approximated as Monash University Clayton Victoria. The disturbance includes tetracycline supplementation as an environmental stress and antibiotic and includes experimental displacement from equilibrium by changing starting proportions. The disturbance is treated as a discrete experimental assay with a defined 7 day duration. The observation window for the resilience assays is seven days with daily measurements.

The study quantifies resilience by simulating node removals in an ecological network using a dynamic disturbance scenario simulation framework and Python NetworkX. It states that disturbance scenarios are simulated by targeting nodes and that nodes become inactive and are removed after a single attack with connected edges removed. It defines resilience indicators as the largest connectivity subgraph S and network efficiency E and states it utilized the score of S as a measure of EN resilience and also combined normalized S and E into an overall resilience degree R. These metrics quantify how much the network is disrupted under attacks which matches resistance as the ability to deviate less from its original state after disturbance rather than recovery. The target variables used for resilience are network metrics S and E and the combined R which are regional network parameters. The disturbance is a simulated attack on network structure so it is model structural. The disturbance is applied as discrete incremental node removals one node at a time until 100 percent removal so it is treated as a pulse sequence. The study area is the urban agglomeration around Hangzhou Bay in Zhejiang Province China with coordinates given as 118•200′ to 123•250′ and 29•13′ to 31•11′ and the average is reported. The first author affiliation is Zhejiang A&F University Hangzhou China. The data used include land use NDVI DEM roads and NPA data for 2020 for the UAHB region so the field country is China. The observation duration is not a time series of repeated measurements over time but a simulation on a static 2020 network so duration is set to zero days. Habitat types include woodland cultivated land water grassland construction land and unused land so multiple habitat types are involved.

The study states that it used a mathematical model and a system of differential equations describing how benthic cover changes over time and it simulated how the fraction of reef space occupied by corals and macroalgae changes following disturbance events. It explicitly defines resilience in the introduction as how ecosystems resist and recover from disturbance. Disturbance is imposed in the model using a kick flow approach where the system is instantaneously perturbed by a kick representing an acute mortality event and then the simulation is resumed which is a pulse disturbance treatment. The analysis includes quantifying the time taken to reach equilibrium for each combination of algal protection and disturbance intensity which is a recovery time measure. The state variables used in the core analysis are proportional cover classes including live coral cover and macroalgal cover and equilibrium cover after disturbance is evaluated which makes the target variable type cover. The modeled system is a coral reef benthic community with corals and macroalgae as key interacting space holders which corresponds to a community level. The disturbance types are implemented as structure removing disturbances that reduce all space holders and structure retaining disturbances that convert live coral to standing dead coral which are structural manipulations in a model. The first author affiliation is University of California Santa Barbara in the United States. The methods describe simulations through time and figures show time series with a disturbance at a time point and trajectories extending to about 10 years so the observation duration is 10 years which is 3650 days. No specific field sampling location coordinates are provided for the resilience quantification because the resilience results are generated from the model rather than from a field time series.

The study repeatedly surveyed woodland bird communities in 24 landscapes near the beginning middle and after an extreme drought and states that it quantified resistance resilience and stability of the avifauna to the decade of drought. Resilience is defined as community change following disturbance focused on the ability to bounce back after a disturbance and quantified as proportional change from mid drought to post drought using Resilience = SRT3/SRT2. This uses observed time separated bird survey data rather than proxies so the measurement is observed. The framework is presented as three parallel measures resistance resilience and stability. Resilience is quantified as proportional change after the drought ended which is a recovery degree measure rather than a rate or time. The target variable used for the resilience calculation is species richness of woodland dependent birds which is a diversity measure and mean incidence is also calculated but the formula shown for resilience is for species richness. The level is community because species richness and mean incidence are community attributes across multiple species. The approach is field observation because the methods describe repeated 30 minute line transect bird surveys across seasons and years. The first author affiliation is Deakin University in Australia. The study area is north central Victoria Australia and the drought is the Millennium Drought so field country is Australia. No coordinates are provided so the location is approximated from north central Victoria and the map indicates a single regional cluster so the range is treated as within two degrees. The region is described as cleared for farming with pastoral cropping and horticulture so the dominant habitat for the landscapes is agricultural. The subjects are birds so the taxon is Animalia. The disturbance is an unmanipulated severe long term drought described as an extreme climatic event and treated as running from 2001 to 2009 with sampling during and after so it is a press disturbance. The observation window spans from early drought sampling in 2002 to post drought sampling in 2011 to 2012 which is about a decade or 3650 days.

The introduction defines resilience as the rate at which a system recovers following disturbance and resistance as the ability of a system to withstand displacement from a disturbance. The methods state that pulses of chlorine were introduced into the streams 24 times over a 12 d period and total residual chlorine was measured after each addition. The methods state that resistance and resilience were measured for ash free dry mass chlorophyll a autotrophic carbon fixation rates bacterial numbers and exoproteolytic activity. The methods state resilience was quantified by fitting a first order model and using the exponential rate of increase r as a measure of resilience which is a recovery rate. The methods also state resilience was assessed by comparing pre disturbance values with recovery values at successive sampling dates using Dunnett test to determine time of recovery to pre disturbance levels which is recovery time. Figures show time series trajectories for AFDM chlorophyll a bacterial numbers carbon fixation and exoproteolytic activity over days after the disturbance. Table 4 reports r values and days to reach pre disturbance levels for multiple parameters. The experiment was conducted in indoor laboratory streams. The first author affiliation is Oak Ridge National Laboratory in Oak Ridge Tennessee USA. The streams were inoculated with rocks collected from Walker Branch on the Oak Ridge reservation in eastern Tennessee. The study subjects for resilience metrics are periphyton structural and functional parameters including algal biomass and carbon fixation. The disturbance is repeated chlorine additions which is a manipulated chemical disturbance treated as pulsed additions.

The study states that it quantified stability as resilience following large perturbations and it defines functional resilience as the recovery rate of total biomass using measurements on days 6 and 21. It also defines compositional resilience as compositional recovery based on Bray Curtis similarity using species abundances on days 6 and 21. These are direct measurements of system response after pesticide exposure so the measurement type is observed. The disturbance is experimentally applied pesticide exposure using chlorpyrifos and linuron described as two types of chemicals so it is manipulated chemical. The perturbation is administered and then recovery is assessed between day 6 and day 21 so the disturbance is treated as a pulse. The experiments lasted for 21 days in 900 mL glass jars with field collected organisms from a freshwater plankton food web representative of Dutch ditches. The first author affiliation is Wageningen University in the Netherlands. The field setting is described as representative of Dutch ditches indicating the Netherlands but no coordinates are provided. The resilience target variables are total biomass and community composition which are community level properties. The paper combines model simulations with experiments so the approach includes modeling and simulation and field experiment.

The abstract defines resilience as rates at which a community recovers after disturbance and the study quantifies functional and compositional recovery after repeated pulse disturbances. The Methods describe a standardized biomass removal experiment with three treatments no removal low disturbance and high disturbance and state this was designed to observe resilience after disturbance. Functional recovery was quantified using community biomass measured as wet weight in grams and open space measured as percent cover of unoccupied space. Compositional recovery was quantified using relative abundances of 13 morphotaxonomic groups and Bray Curtis dissimilarity with PERMANOVA. Recovery rate is supported by the focus on speed of functional recovery and rates of recovery and by the one month recovery period after removals. Recovery degree is supported by planned contrasts comparing disturbed treatments to controls at assessment times and by statements about incomplete recovery at late assembly. The study subjects are sessile marine invertebrate communities including ascidians bryozoans cirripeds tube building worms sponges bivalves and cnidarians. The experiment was conducted in subtidal nearshore marinas on the Pacific coasts of North and Central America at Ketchikan Alaska San Francisco Bay California La Paz Mexico and Panama City Panama with coordinates given for each region. The disturbance is manipulated biomass removal by manual scrapes which is a structural disturbance and it is treated as a pulse disturbance with five discrete removals over one year. The observation window spans one year because removals were conducted over the course of one year and communities were assessed at 3 and 12 months with the full schedule covering 1 year.

The abstract states the model is based on the Pressure-Sensitivity-Resilience framework and the introduction defines PSR components and says vulnerability is a function composed of sub indicators such as pressure sensitivity and resilience. The methods state the PSR framework covers ecological pressure ecological sensitivity and ecological resilience and the study constructs NOEV based on PSR. The resilience layer is quantified using NDVI slope trend analysis and Table 2 lists resilience as the annual maximum NDVI during 1990 to 2020. The introduction says NDVI slope trend analysis is used to characterize the response of vegetation to external pressure and its own resilience and the discussion says the resilience layer is quantified based on the annual NDVI maximum change rate from 1990 to 2020. This uses an indicator to represent resilience rather than measuring recovery after a defined disturbance event so the measurement is inferred. NDVI is a vegetation index used as the base data of the NOEV model and is treated as vegetation condition and productivity related so the target variable type is ecosystem function. The study focuses on natural oasis vegetation dynamics and vulnerability at the oasis scale so the level is ecosystem. Data sources are Landsat and other remote sensing products and field surveys for classification accuracy and biomass weights so the approach is field observation. The first author affiliation is in Beijing China so the institution country is CN. The study area is Dunhuang oasis in Gansu province China so the field country is CN and the coordinates are given as 39.50 to 40.36 N and 93.44 to 95.31 E so the average location is N40.231 E94.638 with a range computed from those bounds. The study is in arid areas with Kumtag Desert expansion and includes Gobi desert transition zones so the dominant habitat is desert. The resilience relates to vegetation so the taxon is Plantae. Because measurement is inferred the disturbance type pattern and observation duration are NA by the instructions.

The introduction defines resilience as capacity to recover following a disturbance and retain processes structures and species composition. The study states it uses a simple definition of resilience as recovery of a response variable to pre fire levels within a time period shorter than the average fire return interval. Methods quantify postfire change by compositing biogeochemical proxies and comparing pre fire baseline 20 to 40 years before each charcoal peak with multiple postfire lags up to 60 to 80 years after each charcoal peak and by classifying pollen samples by time since fire. Results report that biogeochemical proxies returned to baseline or reversed in sign by 60 to 80 years after fire and that pollen assemblages typically returned to pre fire values within about 80 years after fire. Discussion states biogeochemical proxies and pollen assemblages recovered to pre fire values within about 50 to 80 years and that average recovery times were less than the average fire return interval. The disturbance is wildfire inferred from peaks in macroscopic charcoal interpreted as local fire events. Fire events are treated as discrete peaks with postfire windows which matches a pulse disturbance framing. The target variables used to quantify recovery include sediment biogeochemical proxies reflecting ecosystem processes such as nitrogen cycling soil erosion and within lake productivity and pollen assemblage measures reflecting vegetation composition. The study area is Silver Lake Montana USA with coordinates given as 47.360 N 115.566 W. The first author affiliation is University of Montana in the United States. The record spans about 4800 years which is about 1752000 days from first to last measurement in the sediment sequence.

The study defines resistance as the ability to withstand change in abundance or biomass and defines resilience as the ability to return to its previous condition following disturbance and it discusses both together as characterizing ability to withstand disturbance. It uses a simulation modelling approach with an age structured population model and it simulates poison bait quantities applied annually and tracks poison bait mortality and biomass at an annual time step for 50 years. It treats population productivity as an index of resilience and ecosystem size as an index of resistance and evaluates population level response to control. The results quantify outcomes as meeting biomass reduction targets such as a 75% biomass reduction by year 15 and median annual biomass relative to a biomass goal. The target variable used in these resilience related analyses is invader biomass including long term invader biomass and carp biomass in Utah Lake. The focal subject is common carp Cyprinus carpio which is an animal. The disturbance is poison baiting which is a chemical control and it is imposed in the model as annual application maintained through the simulation window. The case study location is Utah Lake UT USA and the map figure identifies Utah Lake in the western United States.

The introduction states that sensitivity and return rate are two key components of ecological stability and that return rate is akin to resilience or elasticity. The methods state that they quantified sensitivity and return rate for phytoplankton in lakes using pulsed nutrient loading as the perturbation and that they monitored total chlorophyll a following standardized nutrient pulses. The methods describe estimating return time as the number of days observed samples were outside the 90% highest posterior density interval and defining return rate as the inverse of return time. This is a direct quantification of recovery dynamics from observed time series so the measurement is observed. The framework is stability = sensitivity + return rate because the abstract and introduction define stability in terms of these two components and equate sensitivity with resistance and return rate with resilience. Quantification is recovery rate because resilience is operationalized as return rate and calculated as the inverse of return time. The target variable used for the resilience quantification is total chlorophyll a which is measured as phytoplankton biomass and treated as a productivity related ecosystem level indicator. The study is a field experiment because they added planktivorous fish and added nutrient pulses to two natural ponds and sampled repeatedly. The study site is Monday Bog and Wednesday Bog near Land O'Lakes Wisconsin USA with coordinates 46 8139N 89 8329W. The disturbance is manipulated resource because nutrients were added as small and large pulses. The disturbance pattern is pulse because the study added discrete nutrient pulses and then monitored recovery. Observation duration is 84 days because the first nutrient pulse was on 6 July 1994 and the last phytoplankton sampling shown in the tables was 16 August 1994 which spans 41 days and in 1995 the first pulse was 12 June and the last listed date is 17 August which spans 66 days and the methods state a 3 to 6 week reference period plus post pulse monitoring and the figure time series cover roughly about 12 weeks.
